title: Pentagon Signs Seven, Excludes Anthropic - TCR 05/02/26
description:
The Pentagon signed classified AI deals with SpaceX, OpenAI, Google, Nvidia, Microsoft, AWS, and Reflection under "any lawful use" while excluding Anthropic, Five Eyes intelligence agencies published joint guidance identifying five categories of agentic AI risk the same week, and the UK AI Security Institute found GPT-5.5 matches Anthropic's restricted Mythos Preview at 71.4% on Expert-level cybersecurity tasks for $1.73 per task.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-2-2026/

0:00 Pentagon signs seven, excludes Anthropic
0:23 Five Eyes joint guidance on agentic AI
4:15 GPT-5.5 matches Mythos on cyber benchmarks
7:10 Musk v. Altman trial testimony
11:18 Vineyard Wind and SoCalGas cost curve inversion
14:20 Meta acquires Assured Robot Intelligence
16:21 What shipped

This episode covers the Pentagon seven-company procurement and Anthropic exclusion, the Five Eyes joint guidance on agentic AI deployment, the GPT-5.5 vs Mythos benchmark convergence, Musk v. Altman trial testimony on the $97.4B xAI bid and OpenAI distillation, Meta's acquisition of Assured Robot Intelligence, the Massachusetts Vineyard Wind $1.4B savings lock-in, and California's SoCalGas hydrogen pipeline rejection.

The Pentagon's seven-company classified procurement and the Five Eyes joint guidance on agentic AI arrived within hours of each other - one half binding seven AI labs to "any lawful use" inside Impact Level 6 and 7 networks, the other half telling every organization deploying agentic AI to assume it will behave unexpectedly and prioritize containment over efficiency. Anthropic's exclusion is the entire shape of the story: eleven weeks after the supply-chain risk designation, the company that refused the lawful-use standard is still standing while reportedly raising at a $900 billion valuation. The UK AISI's evaluation of GPT-5.5 showed Expert-level cybersecurity capability matching Anthropic's restricted Mythos Preview within margin of error, dropping the floor of frontier offensive cyber to $1.73 per task and widening the defender population to every security team with an API budget. In federal court, Jared Birchall acknowledged xAI's $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI's nonprofit assets was a market intervention, and Musk admitted xAI distills OpenAI's models for Grok - the same practice the State Department framed as theft two weeks ago when Chinese labs did it. Meta absorbed humanoid foundation-model startup Assured Robot Intelligence. Massachusetts locked in $1.4 billion in projected savings from Vineyard Wind at 1.4 cents per kWh. California regulators rejected SoCalGas's $266 million hydrogen pipeline planning bill as ratepayer abuse. The cost curves inverted enough that institutional reflexes built inside the gas era are producing decisions the gas era did not anticipate.
